Duluth Event Calendar

Home > Events > Event Calendar

The Fun is Live!

Find What’s On in Duluth

You’ve found the ultimate guide to what’s happening in Duluth, Minnesota! Our city doesn’t believe in boredom: with 12 months of festivals, live music, sports, and fun-filled events, our calendar is packed with extraordinary times all year long. Dive into the calendar below to discover a world of unique experiences waiting for you.

Stay in the Know

Don’t miss out on the latest updates, exciting news, and insider information from Duluth! Signing up for our newsletter is the best way to stay connected and get inspiration delivered right to your inbox. Discover new attractions, upcoming events, dining hotspots, and all the Duluthy happenings that make our city so unique.

Sign up for our newsletter

Duluth Event Calendar

Ready to find your next adventure? Our comprehensive event calendar is packed with happenings, big and small, from today and into the future. Browse by month to discover a wide variety of activities, including music, sports, theater, recreation, and more!

35 events found.

3 events, 1
2026-05-04
Gender Joy Art Show
Gender Joy Art Show
May 4 - June 27
Gender Joy Art Show

The 3rd Annual Gender Joy Art Show is being held from May 4th to June 27th with an opening reception on Thursday, May 7th from 5-7pm. Organized by YWCA Duluth and PAVSA and exhibited in the Zeitgeist atrium and Teatro, this year’s show not only includes artwork from girls, women and gender expansive people but features survivor artwork from PAVSA's weekly Art Group, all representing how each finds joy in their gender. Event attendees will get a chance to express themselves in an interactive community art project hosted by Art of Presence and will have an opportunity to heal through collective action and see their collaborative piece evolve into a finished painting displayed for the remainder of the show. Light refreshments will be offered as well as an opportunity to learn more about YWCA Duluth and PAVSA’s work in our community. Most pieces of artwork will be available for purchase...

Free
6:00 pm - 9:00 pm
High Key Mondays & Industry Nights
June 1 • 6:00 pm - 9:00 pm
High Key Mondays & Industry Nights

High Key Mondays & Industry Night Are BACK! Live local music | Drink specials | 6–9pm High Key Monday is FRI-YAY for many of our industry partners — and we’re roll with it. Calling all restaurant, bar, liquor store, and brewery professionals in the Twin Ports — get on down to the Paddle for Industry Night! Show a worker ID or pay stub for $1 off all Bent Paddle beverages. Thank you for keeping our paddles in the water! Everyone is welcome! Come for the tunes, stay for the conversations, delicious beverages, and good times. This month to kick off and celebrate the return, we invite you to connect with local organizations making waves in the Twin Ports — like WE Health, Trans+ Northland, Twin Ports Artists Collective, and Men As Peacemakers. Org’s may vary based on who available each week, but the good vibes are guaranteed!

Free
7:00 pm - 9:00 pm
Rich Mattson & The Northstars
June 1 • 7:00 pm - 9:00 pm
Rich Mattson & The Northstars

Over the course of his long and illustrious career sprouting from the same teal backwater as Bob Dylan on northern Minnesota’s Iron Range, Rich Mattson came up learning songs from John Denver to Van Halen with a little Elvis Costello and Neil Young tossed into the mix. Add in some heaping handfuls of U2, the Replacements, and CCR and blend until the ingredients come splattering in bits and pieces and you have around 500 tunes and this fella’s discography. Now residing in Sparta with his partner Germaine Gemberling, the two have been writing, recording, and performing together since 2010. Their many albums have received rave reviews and high praise from the indie rock/americana/jangle-core crowd. The Northstars perform all around MN and beyond and have been invited to share the stage with such notable acts as the Built To Spill, Minus 5, the Jayhawks, Drivin’ and Cryin’, and Trampled By Turtles....

Free

Gender Joy Art Show
2026-06-02
$10 Tuesday on the Vista Fleet
$10 Tuesday on the Vista Fleet
June 2
$10 Tuesday on the Vista Fleet

Climb onboard for one of Duluth’s favorite summer traditions, $10 Tuesdays are back at the Vista Fleet! Enjoy live-narrated sightseeing cruises through the Duluth-Superior Harbor for just $10 per ticket, all day long, every Tuesday through Labor Day. Reserve your tickets in advance as tours DO sell out. Take in stunning views of Lake Superior, the Aerial Lift Bridge, and the working waterfront from the comfort of the Vista Fleet. Concessions and a full bar are available onboard, so grab a drink, kick back, and enjoy a midweek escape on the water. Book your tickets online, no promo code needed!

Teen Open Studio (Spring Sessions)

2026-05-04
Gender Joy Art Show
9:00 am - 3:00 pm
Leadership Duluth
June 3 • 9:00 am - 3:00 pm
Leadership Duluth

Leadership Duluth is a comprehensive 10-month leadership development program by the Duluth Area Chamber of Commerce that aims to unite, engage, and empower leaders to foster change within their workplace and community. Throughout the program, participants examine core leadership competencies, learn about Duluth’s cultural and business history, and discuss current issues facing the region. As a result, participants grow to understand the elements of a strong community and how their leadership can create opportunities within a business and cultivate a robust economy. More than 1,500 area professionals have completed the Leadership Duluth program since its first graduating class in 1988. For more information, visit leadershipduluth.com SCHEDULE Dates subject to change Each session will run 9:00 a.m. – 3:00 p.m. September 10, 2025 – Kick-Off September 17, 2025 October 8, 2025 November 12, 2025 December 10, 2025 January 7, 2026 February 4, 2026 March 4 or 11, 2026 April 15, 2026 May...

June 3 • 5:00 pm - 7:00 pm
Weekly Wine Gatherings

Gather with us every Wednesday to explore a curated selection of featured wines in a relaxed atmosphere. Whether you're a seasoned wine enthusiast or simply curious to expand your palate, this is the perfect opportunity to try something new, mingle with fellow wine lovers, and enjoy the cozy elegance of the Sheraton Duluth.

2026-05-04
Gender Joy Art Show
4:00 pm - 7:00 pm
Moments Woven at Siiviis Gallery
June 4 • 4:00 pm - 7:00 pm
Moments Woven at Siiviis Gallery

Ben McGaffey is a representational oil painter and draftsman whose work centers on rediscovering what feels true and worth holding onto. Beauty is to be found in our everyday lives as long as we adjust our focus. Ben has spent the past eight years studying under Jeffrey T. Larson, receiving an education in the rigorous discipline of classical art training. Yet, as with all things learned, it is not the rules but the freedoms that came from them which now compel Ben’s art practice. "My paintings, I hope, reflect this idea: In this age of advancement, where we have mastered science and shed the need for mystery, I find it increasingly evident that we are losing touch with what makes us human. We do not live by the cold, unwavering laws of mathematics or science alone. We are driven by stories, by art, and by beauty that stirs the soul...

6:00 pm - 10:00 pm
Brinn Major, Hiahli & The Lavender Project
June 4 • 6:00 pm - 10:00 pm
Brinn Major, Hiahli & The Lavender Project

June 4th is going to be a fire Thursday in the Yard! Expect vinyl music from Northern Wav Crew between mind blowing sets from Hiahli, The Lavender Project, and Brinn Major. Hiahli A Minneapolis-based live hip-hop band bringing Golden-Age Hip-Hop energy with a full rock n’ roll band setup. Fronted by the high-octane MC Cold Sweat, Hiahli blends grungy boom bap beats, live instrumentation, and cosmic vibes into a show built for good times — and maybe even a trip to the moon. The Lavender Project Hailing from La Crosse, WI, The Lavender Project fuses indie, alternative rock, and hip-hop into immersive soundscapes for MCs Jet Hertz and Fernando Mendoza. Their heartfelt lyrics, captivating freestyle skills, and ever-changing live energy make every performance unique. Brinn Major Local drag performer, rapper, DJ, songwriter, and producer Brinn Major steps into the spotlight. A proud member of the Haus of Queer, Forté will...

Huge UUCD Rummage Sale
Huge UUCD Rummage Sale
June 5 • 3:00 pm - June 7 • 1:00 pm
Huge UUCD Rummage Sale

This church rummage sale features books, furniture, household goods, Sewing and Art supplies, Garden and other tools, Sporting goods, Collectibles and Linens. Starts Friday afternoon and ends Saturday 1 pm. Unitarian Universalist Congregation of Duluth Fundraiser. 835 College Street, between UMD and St. Scholastica.

2:30 pm - 3:30 pm
Glow in the Dark Future Creature Workshop
June 5 • 2:30 pm - 3:30 pm
Glow in the Dark Future Creature Workshop

Jump into the future and make a glow in the dark animal puppet! If you were to travel through space and time, what creatures would you find? Tap into your imagination through the wonders of blacklight and create an amazing critter to take home with you. Recommended for ages 6+.

Cards & Cribbage Night at Hungry Hippie Tacos

Cards & Cribbage Night at Hungry Hippie Tacos

Friday’s Cards & Cribbage is back! Bring your family! Bring your friends! Enjoy comfy, cozy vibes along with $1 off tap beers and free chips and salsa. Join us in conversation and connecting over a hand of cards every Friday 3pm – close at both our Duluth and Grand Marais locations.

Free
10:00 am - 6:00 pm
Photography & Art at the Rock: Voyageur Artists
June 6 • 10:00 am - 6:00 pm
Photography & Art at the Rock: Voyageur Artists

June at the Rock is a celebration of creativity, community, and place. We are proud to feature Voyageur Artists, a collective of local artists from Two Harbors whose work reflects the beauty and character of Minnesota’s North Shore. This group exhibit brings together a diverse range of styles, perspectives, and mediums — all rooted in a shared connection to the landscapes, waters, and communities that define this region. From painting to photography and beyond, each piece offers a unique interpretation of place. Voyageur Artists are dedicated to inspiring creativity within the community through exhibitions, workshops, and educational opportunities. Their work not only celebrates the North Shore, but also supports the next generation of artists through scholarships and outreach. On display June 1–30 Meet the Artists: Saturdays in June | 10 AM – 6 PM Experience the North Shore through many lenses — and meet the artists who call it home.

Free
10:00 am - 12:00 pm
Gun Violence Awareness Event
June 6 • 10:00 am - 12:00 pm
Gun Violence Awareness Event

The Gun Violence Awareness Coalition will be hosting a community event on Saturday June 6 at 10:00 a.m. recognizing National Gun Violence Awareness Day and Wear Orange weekend, when Americans across the country join together to demand an end to gun violence. Our event will feature speakers, tables of information surrounding gun violence prevention and we will have a ceremony remember some of the 130 Americans each day, who lose their lives in the United States, to gun violence. This year, Wear Orange is about more than raising awareness. It's about keeping things practical and local, and giving people real ways to save lives right now. Whether it’s through secure gun storage education or showing up for your community, focus on some of the concrete steps you can take to recommit to a culture of responsibility, safety, and power.

Photography & Art at the Rock: Voyageur Artists

June at the Rock is a celebration of creativity, community, and place. We are proud to feature Voyageur Artists, a collective of local artists from Two Harbors whose work reflects the beauty and character of Minnesota’s North Shore. This group exhibit brings together a diverse range of styles, perspectives, and mediums — all rooted in a shared connection to the landscapes, waters, and communities that define this region. From painting to photography and beyond, each piece offers a unique interpretation of place. Voyageur Artists are dedicated to inspiring creativity within the community through exhibitions, workshops, and educational opportunities. Free
10:30 am - 12:30 pm
Duluth River Train Excursion
June 7 • 10:30 am - 12:30 pm
Duluth River Train Excursion

Ride the Lake Superior and Mississippi Railroad. A historic 2-hour rail journey through the majestic St. Louis River Estuary. Family-friendly, volunteer-run, and purely Duluth. Our narrated tour takes you along the original Lake Superior & Mississippi line in vintage coaches. Go where cars and hiking boots can’t reach. The St. Louis River Estuary is a hidden sanctuary for bald eagles, osprey, and Northland wildlife. Bring your camera for unparalleled views of the Duluth riverfront. Best to arrive about a half-hour before departure. Our ticket office is located just off of Grand Avenue.

11:00 am - 2:00 pm
Carlton County Animal Rescue Adoption Event
June 7 • 11:00 am - 2:00 pm
Carlton County Animal Rescue Adoption Event

Carlton County Animal Rescue will be at various locations throughout Duluth with adoptable animals as well as having volunteers available to answer your questions regarding adoption, volunteer opportunities and the joys of fostering. Please note that we do not do same day adoptions. 2026 Adoption Events Fleet Farm: Jan. 25th from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: Feb. 1st and Feb. 21st from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: Feb. 22nd from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: Mar. 26th from 5–7:30pm. PetSmart: Mar. 29th from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: June 7th from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: June 9th from 5–7:30pm. Tractor Supply: June 14th from 10am - 1pm and June 30th from 4:30-6:30pm. We will also host an adoption event at the Moose Lake Winter Market, 700 5th Street in Moose Lake on Feb. 7th from 9am - 2pm.

9:00 am - 12:00 pm
Digital Art Camp with Ant Nold (Session 1 of 5)
June 8 • 9:00 am - 12:00 pm
Digital Art Camp with Ant Nold (Session 1 of 5)

Explore digital art in this fun five-day camp! Students will learn how artists use tablets to draw, paint, edit images, and create simple animations. A limited number of iPads will be available to use in class, and students are welcome to bring their own tablet. Please select your ticket option accordingly so we can make sure everyone has an iPad. Students bringing their own device should have Procreate, Calipeg, and Sketchbook installed before the first day. Beginners are welcome. Sponsored by the MN State Art Board

Gatherings by Sheraton: Mark Connolly Live!
June 9 • 5:00 pm - 7:00 pm
Gatherings by Sheraton: Mark Connolly Live!

Join us at the Restaurant 301 bar for local live music! A local guitarist and singer, Mark Connolly, is known for his stripped-down, authentic style that puts the heart of the music front and center. His sound is intimate and relatable, and his natural ability to connect with the audience makes every performance feel personal. Come relax, listen, and enjoy a night of great music.

8:00 am - 5:00 pm
Head of the Lakes United Way Summer Day of Caring
June 10 • 8:00 am - 5:00 pm
Head of the Lakes United Way Summer Day of Caring

Join us for Head of the Lakes United Way's Day of Caring on Wednesday, June 11th! Day of Caring is a community-wide event where hundreds of volunteers complete projects for local non-profit agencies. Volunteering is a direct and hands-on effort to help advance the missions of these local organizations. Not only does Day of Caring provide non-profits with valuable resources, it is also a great opportunity to educate volunteers about the important work non-profit organizations do in our community. Recruit your friends, family or co-workers to lend a hand.

Free
4:00 pm - 6:00 pm
Waterfront Plaza Kick-Off
June 10 • 4:00 pm - 6:00 pm
Waterfront Plaza Kick-Off

Celebrate the DECC’s newest venue Wednesday, June 10 4:00 – 6:00 pm East end of Harbor Drive (end closest to Blue Bridge) Featuring Mayor Roger Reinert, the Duluth Chamber, and DECC staff Come explore the new Waterfront Plaza The Waterfront Plaza (Harbor Drive) refresh has been completed. With its completion, we’ve added a new venue to the DECC portfolio of venues. We’re throwing a little party to celebrate! Mayor Roger Reinert will kick off the event with brief remarks. Followed by a ribbon-cutting with the Duluth Area Chamber of Commerce and their ambassadors. Guests will then be encouraged to explore the Waterfront Plaza further to enjoy a charcuterie board curated by Ykre Acres, complimentary mocktails, and a DJ. A wedding setup, in partnership with Lundeen Productions, will be assembled on the Waterfront Plaza. The DECC will serve complimentary mocktails.

Summer Reads for 2026 with MPR News

Park Point Rummage Sale
June 12 • 8:00 am - 5:00 pm
Park Point Rummage Sale

There will be four miles of bargains along Minnesota Avenue during the annual Park Point Rummage Sale. Free parking is available on both sides of the street. Food vendors will be on hand. Avoid congestion by parking at the Beach House on South 48th Street and Minnesota Ave. At the end of the day, catch the Park Point city bus from the bridge back to your car at the Beach House.

CSM John S. Werner Memorial 5K Run-Walk at 9AM on 6/13/2026
June 13 • 9:00 am - 11:00 am
CSM John S. Werner Memorial 5K Run-Walk at 9AM on 6/13/2026

CSM John S. Werner Memorial 5K Run-Walk June 13, 2026 | 9 AM Start & Finish: The Other Place Bar & Grill 3930 E Calvary Rd Duluth, MN 55803 Entry Fee: $35 Calling all runners — sign up online: Register now St. Louis County Historical Society and its Veterans Memorial Hall are holding a 5k Run-Walk to honor the memory of CSM (Command Sergeant Major) John S. Werner on Saturday, June 13, 2026. Start time is 9AM and the opening ceremony is 8:45AM at The Other Place Bar & Grill, 3930 East Calvary Rd, Duluth, MN 55803. Please join us as we honor the life and legacy of CSM John S. Werner. A devoted steward of our nation, state, and region, John was deeply committed to the Society’s mission and the well-being of Northland veterans. He worked steadfastly to preserve the storied lineage of our local service members, ensuring their sacrifices remain...

Photography & Art at the Rock: Voyageur Artists
June 14 • 10:00 am - 6:00 pm
Photography & Art at the Rock: Voyageur Artists

June at the Rock is a celebration of creativity, community, and place. We are proud to feature Voyageur Artists, a collective of local artists from Two Harbors whose work reflects the beauty and character of Minnesota’s North Shore. This group exhibit brings together a diverse range of styles, perspectives, and mediums — all rooted in a shared connection to the landscapes, waters, and communities that define this region. From painting to photography and beyond, each piece offers a unique interpretation of place. Voyageur Artists are dedicated to inspiring creativity within the community through exhibitions, workshops, and educational opportunities. Their work not only celebrates the North Shore, but also supports the next generation of artists through scholarships and outreach. On display June 1–30 Meet the Artists: Saturdays in June | 10 AM – 6 PM Experience the North Shore through many lenses — and meet the artists who call it home.

10:00 am - 5:00 pm
William A. Irvin Family Day
June 14 • 10:00 am - 5:00 pm
William A. Irvin Family Day

The DECC Presents William A Irvin Family Day Sunday, June 14, 2026 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM At the William A Irvin on Harbor Drive during regular tour hours TICKETS Children 17 and under are free with a paying adult, in person at the ticket booth only. Added experiences are free for Tour Guests to enjoy. All ages welcome! Regular parking rates apply Celebrating new, family-friendly tour additions with a Family Fun Day  The William A. Irvin has always been an entertaining place for kids. The go-at-your-own-pace vintage freighter tour has always been a wonderful setting for littles to let their imaginations set sail.  Now, the Irvin crew is adding to that fun.  Guests can enjoy free face painting, Duluth-themed coloring pages, chalk stations, and plenty of fun games throughout the ship. Regular tour rates and parking apply.  The William A. Irvin floating museum summer hours are 10am – 5pm. (last tour...

10:00 am - 1:00 pm
Carlton County Animal Rescue Adoption Event
June 14 • 10:00 am - 1:00 pm
Carlton County Animal Rescue Adoption Event

Carlton County Animal Rescue will be at various locations throughout Duluth with adoptable animals as well as having volunteers available to answer your questions regarding adoption, volunteer opportunities and the joys of fostering. Please note that we do not do same day adoptions. 2026 Adoption Events Fleet Farm: Jan. 25th from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: Feb. 1st and Feb. 21st from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: Feb. 22nd from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: Mar. 26th from 5–7:30pm. PetSmart: Mar. 29th from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: June 7th from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: June 9th from 5–7:30pm. Tractor Supply: June 14th from 10am - 1pm and June 30th from 4:30-6:30pm. We will also host an adoption event at the Moose Lake Winter Market, 700 5th Street in Moose Lake on Feb. 7th from 9am - 2pm.

9:00 am - 12:00 pm
KidsConnect Summer Camp
June 15 • 9:00 am - 12:00 pm
KidsConnect Summer Camp

Calling all K through 5 elementary children! There are still a few spots open! KidsConnect is where children use their brain power as they connect with Jesus, family, and friends of all ages! As children learn and create together, they will find a place of belonging, make new friends, and follow Jesus’s example of making room for all and loving one another. Each day starts with gathering, singing and looking for connections to our communities, families, friends and the divine. Throughout the week we will engage in service projects for our community, such as gathering food for a local food shelf. Then children rotate between 3 different stations: Story Connections (reading the daily Bible story and a Children's Book related to that day's theme); Kinetic Connections (playing fun and cooperative games outside - a chance to move and process feelings and learnings); Creative Connections (wondering and exploration of the daily...

June 16
$10 Tuesday on the Vista Fleet

Climb onboard for one of Duluth’s favorite summer traditions, $10 Tuesdays are back at the Vista Fleet! Enjoy live-narrated sightseeing cruises through the Duluth-Superior Harbor for just $10 per ticket, all day long, every Tuesday through Labor Day. Reserve your tickets in advance as tours DO sell out. Take in stunning views of Lake Superior, the Aerial Lift Bridge, and the working waterfront from the comfort of the Vista Fleet. Concessions and a full bar are available onboard, so grab a drink, kick back, and enjoy a midweek escape on the water. Book your tickets online, no promo code needed!

12:00 pm - 3:00 pm
Antique Appraisal
June 17 • 12:00 pm - 3:00 pm
Antique Appraisal

Bring your antique/collectible item for a free appraisal to the St. Louis County Depot at 506 West Michigan Street, Duluth, Minnesota. The St. Louis County Historical Society has arranged for Denny Mager of Northern Specialty, an expert in antique appraisal, to be at your service beginning at noon in the Union Lounge (outside of the Board Room), which is on street level. Follow event signage. Registration is in person only and cut-off at 2:30PM, so the appraiser can finish appraisals by 3PM. Guests are called up in the order that they are registered. You may bring up to two items per person for an informal oral identification and evaluation of each antique. If the piece is too large or fragile to be hand-carried, clear photographs with a description of any marks and labels will be sufficient. Past participants have thoroughly enjoyed the appraiser’s comments on what is always an interesting...

2026-06-18
Grandma’s Marathon
Grandma’s Marathon
June 18 - June 20
Grandma’s Marathon

Grandma’s Marathon is a running event that draws more than 20,000 runners from around the world each June, winding along the breathtaking shores of Lake Superior before finishing in Duluth’s energetic Canal Park.

2026-06-18
Grandma’s Marathon Activities
Grandma’s Marathon Activities
June 18 - June 20
Grandma’s Marathon Activities

Since their inaugural race in 1977, Grandma’s Marathon has grown into something much bigger than just a 26.2-mile race along the scenic North Shore of Minnesota. It’s now become a “can’t-miss” weekend in June, a moment when participants of all calibers come together to celebrate the vigor of the human spirit. It’s a day the word “impossible” ceases to exist, a day 9,000 people become your best running buddy. So get ready to once again tie up your laces for another year of memories, miles, and human might. Race Packet Pickup Thursday, June 18 | 4 PM to 8 PM Friday, June 19 | 10 AM to 11 PM Essentia Health Fitness Expo Thursday, June 18 | 4 PM to 8 PM Friday, June 19 | 10 AM to 8 PM Admission: Free Michelina’s All-You-Can-Eat Spaghetti Dinner Time: 11 AM to 9 PM, Friday, June 19 Cost: Adult — $20...

1:30 pm - 7:00 pm
Rock the Bayfront Festival
June 19 • 1:30 pm - 7:00 pm
Rock the Bayfront Festival

When the race is over, the fun’s just beginning on Grandma’s Marathon weekend! With a move to Duluth’s Bayfront Festival Park the party’s as big and as fun as ever for our participants, volunteers, supporters, and the entire community! There’s something for everyone at Rock the Bayfront – families and kids included – starting Friday afternoon and going strong through Saturday night, with live music and entertainment at Bayfront Festival Park just beside the waters of the Duluth Harbor. Whether you’re in need of space to lay out with the family following your big run or a just a fun night out with friends, Grandma’s Marathon weekend has got you covered. You may come for the run, but you’ll stay for the fun! LINEUP Friday GB Leighton | 1:30 pm – 3:30 pm Hippo Campus | 5:30 pm – 7:00 pm Saturday Boxcar | 9:00 am – 10:30 am Not Fade...

8:00 am - 12:00 pm
Bacon & Bloodies: Grandma’s Marathon Race Viewing Bar
June 20 • 8:00 am - 12:00 pm
Bacon & Bloodies: Grandma’s Marathon Race Viewing Bar

Saturday, June 20 | 8:00 am – 12:00 pm Open to the public | Cash Bar | Free to attend | Regular parking rates apply Guests can enter via Entrance D (by the traffic circle). Imagine holding a Bloody Mary with a healthy strip of bacon without having to fight the crowds. This is a VIP way to celebrate Grandma’s Marathon morning. Join us to cheer on the Grandma Marathon runners from the perfect perch of the Harbor Roof and Symphony Hall decks. Overlooking Harbor Drive – with a backdrop of Canal Park and the Duluth Aerial Lift Bridge – it’s a beautiful spot for an early summer day. And given that Harbor Drive is one of the last legs of the race and runners truly appreciate your cheers. It’s almost a public service! Bloody Marys, local beer, wine, and soft drinks will be available for purchase at the cash...

9 events, 21
2026-05-04
Gender Joy Art Show
10:00 am - 6:00 pm
Photography & Art at the Rock: Voyageur Artists
June 21 • 10:00 am - 6:00 pm
Photography & Art at the Rock: Voyageur Artists

June at the Rock is a celebration of creativity, community, and place. We are proud to feature Voyageur Artists, a collective of local artists from Two Harbors whose work reflects the beauty and character of Minnesota’s North Shore. This group exhibit brings together a diverse range of styles, perspectives, and mediums — all rooted in a shared connection to the landscapes, waters, and communities that define this region. From painting to photography and beyond, each piece offers a unique interpretation of place. Voyageur Artists are dedicated to inspiring creativity within the community through exhibitions, workshops, and educational opportunities. Their work not only celebrates the North Shore, but also supports the next generation of artists through scholarships and outreach. On display June 1–30 Meet the Artists: Saturdays in June | 10 AM – 6 PM Experience the North Shore through many lenses — and meet the artists who call it home.

Free
10:30 am - 12:30 pm
Duluth River Train Excursion
June 21 • 10:30 am - 12:30 pm
Duluth River Train Excursion

Ride the Lake Superior and Mississippi Railroad. A historic 2-hour rail journey through the majestic St. Louis River Estuary. Family-friendly, volunteer-run, and purely Duluth. Our narrated tour takes you along the original Lake Superior & Mississippi line in vintage coaches. Go where cars and hiking boots can’t reach. The St. Louis River Estuary is a hidden sanctuary for bald eagles, osprey, and Northland wildlife. Bring your camera for unparalleled views of the Duluth riverfront. Best to arrive about a half-hour before departure. Our ticket office is located just off of Grand Avenue.

11:00 am - 12:30 pm
Father’s Day BBQ Lunch Train
June 21 • 11:00 am - 12:30 pm
Father’s Day BBQ Lunch Train

Sunday, June 21, 2026: 11am or 1pm Enjoy some delicious BBQ with Dad on a train excursion. Seating in our dining car, with a dome-lounge available after lunch to enjoy the return trip. Two trips this day, a shorter trip in the morning, or a longer ride in the afternoon. Same menu on both. Tickets available at www.DuluthTrains.com

$60 – $75

Gender Joy Art Show
Gender Joy Art Show
12:00 am
Minnesota Monsters vs Michigan Arsenal
June 22 • 12:00 am
Minnesota Monsters vs Michigan Arsenal

Arena Football One at AMSOIL Arena Feel the Roar. Live the Legacy. The Minnesota Monsters are more than a football team, they’re a movement built on passion, power, and community pride. Based in Duluth and representing the Twin Ports, the Monsters deliver high-impact football and unforgettable entertainment every summer. From the first kickoff to the final whistle, their mission is simple: Bring fans together through elite competition, live entertainment, and nonstop energy.   TICKETS Tickets on sale now! Group sales: https://mnmonsters.com/contact/   Learn more about the Minnesota Monsters Learn more about Arena Football One   AMSOIL Arena Know-Before-You-Go

9:30 am - 11:30 am
Forest Bathing at the Lake Superior Zoo
June 22 • 9:30 am - 11:30 am
Forest Bathing at the Lake Superior Zoo

Monday, June 22, 2026 from 9:30-11:30 a.m. Explore and be inspired while forest bathing at the Lake Superior Zoo. Different from a nature hike, being fully in the present moment gently awakens awe and discovery. Technology is set aside to plug into the healing and calming power of nature. During the 2 hour session a series of guided invitations are intermixed with walking slowly, sitting in silence, mindfully observing nature, and sharing what is witnessed. There is a mix of solo and group time. The session concludes with tea and light snacks. Return home to your place of belonging as part of what is wonderfully wild. $40 per person, or $70 for one youth and accompanying adult. Pre-registration with payment required.

$40

8 events, 24
2026-05-04
Gender Joy Art Show
9:00 am - 3:00 pm
Leadership Duluth
June 24 • 9:00 am - 3:00 pm
Leadership Duluth

Leadership Duluth is a comprehensive 10-month leadership development program by the Duluth Area Chamber of Commerce that aims to unite, engage, and empower leaders to foster change within their workplace and community. Throughout the program, participants examine core leadership competencies, learn about Duluth’s cultural and business history, and discuss current issues facing the region. As a result, participants grow to understand the elements of a strong community and how their leadership can create opportunities within a business and cultivate a robust economy. More than 1,500 area professionals have completed the Leadership Duluth program since its first graduating class in 1988. For more information, visit leadershipduluth.com SCHEDULE Dates subject to change Each session will run 9:00 a.m. – 3:00 p.m. September 10, 2025 – Kick-Off September 17, 2025 October 8, 2025 November 12, 2025 December 10, 2025 January 7, 2026 February 4, 2026 March 4 or 11, 2026 April 15, 2026 May...

Free
9:30 am - 10:30 am
Storytime on the Irvin
June 24 • 9:30 am - 10:30 am
Storytime on the Irvin

Hosted by the Duluth Public Library Storytime ho! Climb aboard the William A. Irvin historical ship for a special storytime! We’ll meet on the top deck for stories, songs, and rhymes at 9:30am, followed by time for families to explore the ship. For kids pre-K – grade 3 with their caregivers. Due to its historical nature as a retired freighter, the Irvin is not ADA accessible and includes multiple staircases to access certain locations. No strollers or wagons please. The top deck has minimal barriers, children must be monitored at all times. When you register, please indicate how many people will be in your group. Ship deck capacity is 130 people. Parking will be free for this event in the DECC parking lot across from the ship. Please let parking booth attendant know you are here for Storytime on the Irvin and they will honor complimentary parking. Register and learn...

Free
5:00 pm - 7:00 pm
Weekly Wine Gatherings
June 24 • 5:00 pm - 7:00 pm
Weekly Wine Gatherings

Gather with us every Wednesday to explore a curated selection of featured wines in a relaxed atmosphere. Whether you're a seasoned wine enthusiast or simply curious to expand your palate, this is the perfect opportunity to try something new, mingle with fellow wine lovers, and enjoy the cozy elegance of the Sheraton Duluth.

5:00 pm - 7:30 pm
Cookies on Tap
June 25 • 5:00 pm - 7:30 pm
Cookies on Tap

Join us for a fun, unique fundraiser focused on local craft food and drink! Cookies on Tap is an adult-only fundraiser to support Girl Scouting in the Twin Ports region. Enjoy samples of Girl Scout Cookie-inspired beer, cider, and treats created by 8-10 of your favorite local breweries, cideries, and restaurants. Complete with live music, a silent auction, and an easygoing vibe, this event will be the highlight of your week! Funds raised support Girl Scout leadership programming for hundreds of local Girl Scouts.

$40
6:00 pm - 8:00 pm
Prime Time at the Paddle: Meat Raffle & More
June 25 • 6:00 pm - 8:00 pm
Prime Time at the Paddle: Meat Raffle & More

Join us at Bent Paddle for Prime Time at The Paddle, hosted by Skyline Rotary, a vintage tradition with a modern twist, all in support of a great cause. All proceeds from fundraising efforts go directly to the Rotary. Enjoy happy hour beverage specials, classic raffle fun, and plenty of chances to win loads of prizes. 7–9 PM | Every 4th Thursday of the Month Retro vibes. Prime Meats. Pure luck. Meat Raffle (vegan options available) Wheel Spin 50/50 Raffles Hosted by Rotary Open to the public event: We simply ask that you order beverages throughout the night and take good care of your friendly Beertenders! Meat at the paddle for a great cause and good time and if you’re lucky, you won’t leave empty handed!

Gender Joy Art Show
11:00 am - 3:00 pm
Science on Deck
June 26 • 11:00 am - 3:00 pm
Science on Deck

The Scientists and Staff of UMD's Large Lakes Observatory (LLO) invite the community to board and tour the Blue Heron Research Vessel. Science on Deck events are free and open to the public from 11 am–3 pm. Enjoy guided tours of the research vessel from the crew, interact with graduate students working on freshwater research, and explore engaging displays. The science presentation at 1:30 pm on June 26 will be from our Large Lakes Observatory's Director, Dr. Catherine O'Reilly, titled: "Underwater Soundscapes." The R/V Blue Heron will tie-up at the Pier B Resort for this event, 800 W. Railroad Street, Duluth, MN. ABOUT LLO: Located on the Duluth- campus the the University of Minnesota Duluth, LLO is the only institute in the country dedicated to the study of large lakes throughout the world. They focus on the global implications of investigations in the areas of aquatic chemistry, circular dynamics, geochemistry,...

Free
5:30 pm - 8:00 pm
Famous Dave’s Summer BBQ Train
June 26 • 5:30 pm - 8:00 pm
Famous Dave’s Summer BBQ Train

Fridays and Saturdays, May–July, 5:30 pm Enjoy some delicious BBQ. Tickets available at www.DuluthTrains.com.

$70
7:30 pm - 9:30 pm
Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior
June 26 • 7:30 pm - 9:30 pm
Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior

Experience Duluth from the water aboard a relaxing Sunset Dinner Cruise with Vista Fleet. Cruise through the Twin Ports harbor while taking in narrated highlights, harbor activity, and panoramic views of Duluth’s iconic skyline and the shores of Lake Superior. Guests will enjoy a freshly prepared dinner catered by Black Woods Catering featuring your choice of entrée: Tuscan Chicken, Roasted Pork Tenderloin, or Butternut Squash Ravioli. Dinner also includes Black Woods House Salad, dinner rolls, Butter & Chive Whipped Potatoes, and Sautéed Green Beans Almondine. Whether you're celebrating a special occasion, entertaining out-of-town guests, or simply looking for a memorable night on the water, the Sunset Dinner Cruise offers one of Duluth’s most unique dining experiences. Don’t forget to add dessert to your reservation for a sweet finish to the evening. Cruises depart from the Duluth waterfront aboard the Vista Fleet.

$30 – $85

Park Point Art Fair
June 27 • 10:00 am - 5:00 pm
Park Point Art Fair

The Park Point Art Fair (PPAF) is a weekend celebration of fine art, creativity, and community. Over 100 juried artists showcase work while visitors enjoy live music, food trucks, hands-on art activities, and an accessible layout. Proceeds support local youth, environmental efforts, and civic projects.

Ride the Lake Superior and Mississippi Railroad. A historic 2-hour rail journey through the majestic St. Louis River Estuary. Family-friendly, volunteer-run, and purely Duluth. Our narrated tour takes you along the original Lake Superior & Mississippi line in vintage coaches. Go where cars and hiking boots can’t reach. The St. Louis River Estuary is a hidden sanctuary for bald eagles, osprey, and Northland wildlife. Bring your camera for unparalleled views of the Duluth riverfront. Best to arrive about a half-hour before departure. Our ticket office is located just off of Grand Avenue.

+ 4 More
5 events, 28
10:00 am - 5:00 pm
Park Point Art Fair
June 28 • 10:00 am - 5:00 pm
Park Point Art Fair

The Park Point Art Fair (PPAF) is a weekend celebration of fine art, creativity, and community. Over 100 juried artists showcase work while visitors enjoy live music, food trucks, hands-on art activities, and an accessible layout. Proceeds support local youth, environmental efforts, and civic projects.

Free
10:30 am - 12:30 pm
Duluth River Train Excursion
June 28 • 10:30 am - 12:30 pm
Duluth River Train Excursion

Ride the Lake Superior and Mississippi Railroad. A historic 2-hour rail journey through the majestic St. Louis River Estuary. Family-friendly, volunteer-run, and purely Duluth. Our narrated tour takes you along the original Lake Superior & Mississippi line in vintage coaches. Go where cars and hiking boots can’t reach. The St. Louis River Estuary is a hidden sanctuary for bald eagles, osprey, and Northland wildlife. Bring your camera for unparalleled views of the Duluth riverfront. Best to arrive about a half-hour before departure. Our ticket office is located just off of Grand Avenue.

1:00 pm - 3:30 pm
Similar Dogs at Ritual Marketplace
June 28 • 1:00 pm - 3:30 pm
Similar Dogs at Ritual Marketplace

Join Us for Similar Sundays from 1pm - 3:30pm at Ritual Marketplace with the Similar Dogs (@similardoggy). Joe Lindzius and Sarah Jane Hale perform their diverse acoustic repertoire. Food and drink specials. Great company. It doesn’t get more magical than this!

Free
+ 2 More
1 event, 29
6:00 pm - 9:00 pm
High Key Mondays & Industry Nights
June 29 • 6:00 pm - 9:00 pm
High Key Mondays & Industry Nights

High Key Mondays & Industry Night Are BACK! Live local music | Drink specials | 6–9pm High Key Monday is FRI-YAY for many of our industry partners — and we’re roll with it. Calling all restaurant, bar, liquor store, and brewery professionals in the Twin Ports — get on down to the Paddle for Industry Night! Show a worker ID or pay stub for $1 off all Bent Paddle beverages. Thank you for keeping our paddles in the water! Everyone is welcome! Come for the tunes, stay for the conversations, delicious beverages, and good times. This month to kick off and celebrate the return, we invite you to connect with local organizations making waves in the Twin Ports — like WE Health, Trans+ Northland, Twin Ports Artists Collective, and Men As Peacemakers. Org’s may vary based on who available each week, but the good vibes are guaranteed!

Free
3 events, 30
2026-06-30
$10 Tuesday on the Vista Fleet
$10 Tuesday on the Vista Fleet
June 30
$10 Tuesday on the Vista Fleet

Climb onboard for one of Duluth’s favorite summer traditions, $10 Tuesdays are back at the Vista Fleet! Enjoy live-narrated sightseeing cruises through the Duluth-Superior Harbor for just $10 per ticket, all day long, every Tuesday through Labor Day. Reserve your tickets in advance as tours DO sell out. Take in stunning views of Lake Superior, the Aerial Lift Bridge, and the working waterfront from the comfort of the Vista Fleet. Concessions and a full bar are available onboard, so grab a drink, kick back, and enjoy a midweek escape on the water. Book your tickets online, no promo code needed!

$10
4:30 pm - 6:30 pm
Carlton County Animal Rescue Adoption Event
June 30 • 4:30 pm - 6:30 pm
Carlton County Animal Rescue Adoption Event

Carlton County Animal Rescue will be at various locations throughout Duluth with adoptable animals as well as having volunteers available to answer your questions regarding adoption, volunteer opportunities and the joys of fostering. Please note that we do not do same day adoptions. 2026 Adoption Events Fleet Farm: Jan. 25th from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: Feb. 1st and Feb. 21st from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: Feb. 22nd from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: Mar. 26th from 5–7:30pm. PetSmart: Mar. 29th from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: June 7th from 11am - 2pm. PetSmart: June 9th from 5–7:30pm. Tractor Supply: June 14th from 10am - 1pm and June 30th from 4:30-6:30pm. We will also host an adoption event at the Moose Lake Winter Market, 700 5th Street in Moose Lake on Feb. 7th from 9am - 2pm.

5:00 pm - 7:00 pm
Teen Open Studio (Spring Sessions)
June 30 • 5:00 pm - 7:00 pm
Teen Open Studio (Spring Sessions)

Explore your creativity in a laid-back atmosphere. Bring your headphones and creative ideas — art supplies provided, but bringing your own is also welcome. Registration covers all sessions, but attending every session is not necessary. Funded by the Minnesota State Arts Board

$26 – $50
4 events, 1
5:00 pm - 7:00 pm
Weekly Wine Gatherings
July 1 • 5:00 pm - 7:00 pm
Weekly Wine Gatherings

Gather with us every Wednesday to explore a curated selection of featured wines in a relaxed atmosphere. Whether you're a seasoned wine enthusiast or simply curious to expand your palate, this is the perfect opportunity to try something new, mingle with fellow wine lovers, and enjoy the cozy elegance of the Sheraton Duluth.

Free
5:30 pm - 8:00 pm
Concerts on the Pier
July 1 • 5:30 pm - 8:00 pm
Concerts on the Pier

Plan Now For A Perfect Summer Night at Glensheen Celebrating its 11th anniversary in 2026, the Concerts on the Pier series is a FREE event every Wednesday, July – August. Bring a lawn chair or explore the grounds to find the perfect spot to enjoy music by local favorites. Come by car, foot or watercraft – all people and ages are welcome! Food and beverages are available for purchase. The event is free and open to the public from 5:30 to 8 pm. Gates open at 5 pm, however, for those interested, early access can be obtained by purchasing grounds admission or a tour. Each night, the opener will perform from 5:30-6 pm and the headliner will play from 6:30-8 pm. At Concerts, guests can connect with neighbors and friends, play lawn games, grab some grub from a local food truck, enjoy a refreshing drink, eat locally-made ice cream, and...

Free
6:30 pm - 8:30 pm
Lake Superior Live Cruise ft. Abe Curran
July 1 • 6:30 pm - 8:30 pm
Lake Superior Live Cruise ft. Abe Curran

Relax on Lake Superior with live local music by Abe Curran, great food, and unforgettable waterfront views. Lake Superior Live is Vista Fleet's summer music series, combining a casual harbor cruise, live performances from some of the Twin Ports' favorite musicians, and a BBQ-style dinner. Whether you're looking for a unique date night, a fun evening with friends, or simply an excuse to get out on the water, this cruise offers a laid-back way to enjoy Duluth's waterfront. Weather permitting, cruises travel through both the Duluth Canal and Superior Entry, offering incredible views of Lake Superior, working waterfront activity, and the Duluth skyline. Your ticket includes a casual BBQ dinner. Grab a drink from the bar, find your favorite seat, and enjoy an evening of live music as the sun begins to set over the harbor. A full bar is available onboard with beer, wine, cocktails, soft drinks, and non-alcoholic...

$2 – $51
+ 1 More
0 events, 2
3 events, 3
12:00 am - 11:00 pm
HAIRBALL – Balls to the Walls Tour 2026
July 3 • 12:00 am - 11:00 pm
HAIRBALL – Balls to the Walls Tour 2026

Presented by Winterfell Management   TICKETS Tickets on sale now!   ABOUT THE SHOW Hairball is going Balls to the Wall in 2026 with a tour that cranks the arena rock experience into overdrive. For over 25 years, Hairball has blown away fans with spot-on recreations of rock’s greatest anthems, and this year is no exception. Hairball features dead on recreations of KISS, AC/DC, Twisted Sister, Motley Crue, Guns N Roses, Bon Jovi, Queen, and more! Expect lights, fire, smoke, and the ultimate Rock & Roll party that has to be seen to be believed. Don’t miss your chance to live the madness!   ABOUT HAIRBALL The Bombastic Celebration of Arena Rock IT’S LIKE 20 CONCERTS IN ONE NIGHT! A band puts on a concert – Hairball puts on an event! Hairball is a Rock & Roll experience you won’t soon forget. The lights, sound, smoke, fire, bombs, and screaming...

5:30 pm - 8:00 pm
Famous Dave’s Summer BBQ Train
July 3 • 5:30 pm - 8:00 pm
Famous Dave’s Summer BBQ Train

Fridays and Saturdays, May–July, 5:30 pm Enjoy some delicious BBQ. Tickets available at www.DuluthTrains.com.

$70
7:30 pm - 9:30 pm
Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior
July 3 • 7:30 pm - 9:30 pm
Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior

Experience Duluth from the water aboard a relaxing Sunset Dinner Cruise with Vista Fleet. Cruise through the Twin Ports harbor while taking in narrated highlights, harbor activity, and panoramic views of Duluth’s iconic skyline and the shores of Lake Superior. Guests will enjoy a freshly prepared dinner catered by Black Woods Catering featuring your choice of entrée: Tuscan Chicken, Roasted Pork Tenderloin, or Butternut Squash Ravioli. Dinner also includes Black Woods House Salad, dinner rolls, Butter & Chive Whipped Potatoes, and Sautéed Green Beans Almondine. Whether you're celebrating a special occasion, entertaining out-of-town guests, or simply looking for a memorable night on the water, the Sunset Dinner Cruise offers one of Duluth’s most unique dining experiences. Don’t forget to add dessert to your reservation for a sweet finish to the evening. Cruises depart from the Duluth waterfront aboard the Vista Fleet.

$30 – $85
3 events, 4
10:30 am - 12:30 pm
Duluth River Train Excursion
July 4 • 10:30 am - 12:30 pm
Duluth River Train Excursion

Ride the Lake Superior and Mississippi Railroad. A historic 2-hour rail journey through the majestic St. Louis River Estuary. Family-friendly, volunteer-run, and purely Duluth. Our narrated tour takes you along the original Lake Superior & Mississippi line in vintage coaches. Go where cars and hiking boots can’t reach. The St. Louis River Estuary is a hidden sanctuary for bald eagles, osprey, and Northland wildlife. Bring your camera for unparalleled views of the Duluth riverfront. Best to arrive about a half-hour before departure. Our ticket office is located just off of Grand Avenue.

12:00 pm - 11:00 pm
City of Duluth Fourth Fest
July 4 • 12:00 pm - 11:00 pm
City of Duluth Fourth Fest

Celebrate Independence Day at Bayfront Festival Park with Duluth Fourth Fest — the region’s biggest Fourth of July party and fireworks show. This free, family-friendly event kicks off at 4pm with food vendors, local crafts, and live music throughout the evening.

Free

6 events, 5
12:00 am
Minnesota Monsters vs Kentucky Barrels
July 5 • 12:00 am
Minnesota Monsters vs Kentucky Barrels

Arena Football One at AMSOIL Arena Feel the Roar. Live the Legacy. The Minnesota Monsters are more than a football team, they’re a movement built on passion, power, and community pride. Based in Duluth and representing the Twin Ports, the Monsters deliver high-impact football and unforgettable entertainment every summer. From the first kickoff to the final whistle, their mission is simple: Bring fans together through elite competition, live entertainment, and nonstop energy.   TICKETS Tickets on sale now! Group sales: https://mnmonsters.com/contact/   Learn more about the Minnesota Monsters Learn more about Arena Football One   AMSOIL Arena Know-Before-You-Go

Want to Add Your Event?

If you have an exciting public event coming up in Duluth, we want to help you spread the word! Submit your event details through our online form for inclusion on our comprehensive event calendar. It’s the perfect way to reach thousands of locals and visitors alike who are looking for what’s happening in the Northland.

Submit Your Event

Get the Planning Resources You Need

Looking to host an event yourself? Duluth is an incredible destination for gatherings of all sizes. Find the resources you need to plan a truly unforgettable meeting, convention, wedding, and more events in our city.

Events in a Superior Setting

Planning a corporate meeting, convention, or sports event? Explore Duluth’s state-of-the-art venues, comprehensive services, and unique atmosphere that ensure a memorable and successful gathering.

Plan Your Duluth Event

Dream Weddings by the Big Lake

From breathtaking waterfront ceremonies to elegant receptions, Duluth offers a truly romantic and unique setting for your wedding. Find venues, vendors, and inspiration to bring your dream day to life.

Say I Do to Duluth

Streamline Your Event Planning

Ready to bring your event to Duluth? Submit your Request for Proposal (RFP) directly to our team. We’ll craft a customized proposal with venue options and service recommendations to get you started!

Submit Your RFP
Visitor Center
522 S. Lake Ave
Duluth, MN 55802

Sales Office
230 W. Superior St., Suite 150
Duluth, MN 55802

(218) 722-4011
cvb@visitduluth.com
